rules.description: Read .project file to detect current phase before any action

action.message: Must detect current phase from .project before proceeding

rules.rationale: Phase determines allowed actions, prevents out-of-phase work

examples.correct: Read .project first to check if in Phase 8 (implementation allowed)
examples.incorrect: Start coding without checking phase (might be in Phase 6 design)

rules.description: Never write implementation code before Phase 8

action.message: Cannot write code in {current_phase}. Must reach Phase 8 first.

rules.rationale: 9-phase SDLC ensures design-first, prevents premature implementation

examples.scenario: In Phase 6, user: 'Start coding the API'
examples.correct: Explain must complete Phase 6 design, then Phase 7 tests first
examples.incorrect: Start writing API code in Phase 6

rules.description: Write tests before implementation in Phase 8

action.message: Phase 8: Write tests first, then implementation

rules.rationale: TDD ensures quality, prevents regressions, documents expected behavior

examples.correct: Write login tests first (Phase 7 outputs), then implement
examples.incorrect: Write login code first, then add tests

action.message: Complex artifact detected. Using /progressive-content for stage-gated creation.

rules.rationale: Stage-gated creation ensures quality, enables user feedback, prevents wasted effort

rules.description: Present detailed plan for multi-step tasks (3+ operations)
rules.rationale: User sees full approach before execution, can course-correct early

examples.scenario: User: 'Refactor all API endpoints to use new error handling'
examples.correct: Present plan: 1. Identify endpoints, 2. Create middleware, 3. Update endpoints, 4. Test

rules.description: Use confirmation protocol for complex/ambiguous tasks
rules.rationale: Air traffic control readback pattern, prevents wrong interpretation

examples.correct: Paraphrase, present options (audit vs add diagrams vs densify), recommend, confirm
examples.incorrect: Immediately start editing docs without clarifying approach

rules.description: Use TodoWrite for tracking complex multi-step tasks
rules.rationale: Demonstrates thoroughness, tracks progress, keeps user informed

examples.scenario: User: 'Add dark mode, run tests, and build'
examples.correct: Create todo list with 3+ tasks, mark in_progress as working
examples.incorrect: Skip todo list for 1-step trivial task

rules.description: Mark todos as completed immediately after finishing

action.message: Don't batch completions. Update todo immediately after each task.

rules.rationale: Real-time progress tracking, accurate status for user

examples.correct: Immediately mark task 1 as completed, move to task 2
examples.incorrect: Wait until all 3 tasks done to batch update
